questions.question: Explain the architecture difference between Apache and Nginx web servers.
keyPoints: Apache uses process/thread-based (MPM) model where each request is handled by a separate process or thread
Nginx uses event-driven, asynchronous, non-blocking architecture
Nginx handles multiple connections in a single thread using events
Apache creates new process/thread per connection; Nginx uses worker processes with event loops

questions.question: What is Virtual Hosting? Explain its types in web servers.
keyPoints: Virtual Hosting allows hosting multiple websites on a single web server
Name-based virtual hosting: Multiple domains share same IP address
IP-based virtual hosting: Each website has a different IP address
Apache uses VirtualHost directive to configure virtual hosts
Nginx uses server block directive for virtual hosting

questions.question: Describe the purpose and working of .htaccess file in Apache.
keyPoints: .htaccess is a per-directory configuration file
Allows directory-level access control, URL rewriting, custom error pages
Changes take effect immediately without restarting server
Used for: password protection, rewrite rules, directory listing, MIME types
Can override main server configuration for specific directories

questions.question: Explain the LAMP stack and its components.
keyPoints: LAMP is a popular open source web development platform
Linux: Operating system as the foundation
Apache: Web server software for serving web pages
MySQL: Relational database for storing data
PHP: Server-side scripting language for dynamic content
All components are free and open source, widely used for web applications

questions.question: What is Reverse Proxy? How does it improve web server performance?
keyPoints: Reverse proxy sits between clients and backend servers
Forwards client requests to appropriate backend servers
Benefits: Load balancing, caching static content, SSL termination, security
Reduces load on backend servers by handling certain requests internally
Provides single entry point for multiple servers

questions.question: Explain different Apache Multi-Processing Modules (MPMs).
keyPoints: Prefork MPM: Pre-forks child processes, each handles one connection, thread-safe
Worker MPM: Hybrid multi-process multi-threaded, more memory efficient
Event MPM: Similar to Worker but handles keep-alive better, designed for high concurrency
Choice of MPM affects performance and thread-safety requirements

questions.question: What is the difference between HTTP and HTTPS protocols?
keyPoints: HTTP: HyperText Transfer Protocol - transfers data in plain text
HTTPS: HTTP Secure - uses SSL/TLS encryption
HTTPS provides: Data encryption, Server authentication, Data integrity
HTTPS uses port 443; HTTP uses port 80
Essential for secure transactions and sensitive data

questions.question: Compare Apache and Nginx web servers in detail, including architecture, performance, configuration, and use cases.
keyPoints: Architecture: Apache uses process/thread-based; Nginx uses event-driven non-blocking
Performance: Nginx faster for static content and high concurrency; Apache better for dynamic content with modules
Configuration: Apache uses .htaccess for per-directory config; Nginx uses centralized nginx.conf
Module System: Apache has 60+ built-in modules; Nginx modules must be compiled
Use Cases: Nginx ideal for static sites, reverse proxy, load balancer; Apache better for .htaccess, dynamic content with PHP
Memory: Nginx uses less memory per connection; Apache more memory intensive
Both can be used together: Nginx as reverse proxy in front of Apache

questions.question: Explain the steps to configure Apache HTTP Server for a basic website with virtual hosting and SSL.
keyPoints: Install Apache: sudo apt-get install apache2
Configure DocumentRoot: Set directory path for website files
Create VirtualHost: Use <VirtualHost> directive with ServerName and DocumentRoot
Enable site: sudo a2ensite and restart Apache
SSL Configuration: Enable mod_ssl using a2enmod ssl
Generate self-signed certificate or obtain CA certificate
Configure SSL VirtualHost with SSLCertificateFile and SSLCertificateKeyFile
Restart Apache to apply changes
Test configuration using apache2ctl configtest

questions.question: Describe the request processing flow in a web server from client request to server response.
keyPoints: 1. Client (browser) initiates connection using HTTP/HTTPS
2. DNS resolves domain name to server IP address
3. Client sends HTTP request to server on port 80/443
4. Server daemon (httpd/nginx) receives request
5. Server parses HTTP request (method, URL, headers)
6. Checks request against configuration and security rules
7. Locates requested resource (static file or executes dynamic script)
8. Backend processes request (PHP, CGI, database query)
9. Server generates HTTP response with status code and content
10. Response sent back to client over established connection
11. Connection closed (or kept alive for keep-alive)
12. Client renders the received content

questions.question: Explain Load Balancing and its techniques in web servers. How does Nginx perform load balancing?
keyPoints: Load Balancing distributes traffic across multiple servers
Benefits: Improved reliability, fault tolerance, scalability, performance
Load Balancing Algorithms: Round Robin, Least Connections, IP Hash, Weighted
Nginx Load Balancing Types: upstream module configuration
Methods: HTTP, HTTPS, FastCGI, uwsgi, scgi, memcached
Nginx directives: upstream, server, location, proxy_pass, ip_hash
Health checks: Mark servers as up/down based on failures
Can include backup servers for failover
Nginx acts as reverse proxy with load balancing
